name: nfsstat
explanation: The Nfsstat check has no required configuration fields and no remote endpoint. It runs the local `nfsiostat` binary as a subprocess (shipped with the Agent at `/opt/datadog-agent/embedded/sbin/nfsiostat`, falling back to `/usr/local/sbin/nfsiostat`, or a user-provided path via `nfsiostat_path`) and parses its tabular output. Discovery on the Agent host reduces to: `is the nfsiostat binary present and does the host have any NFS mount points?`. This is pattern L (local CLI subprocess). The check raises a warning (not an error) when no NFS mounts are found, so naive auto-enabling could produce noise on hosts without NFS — auto-config should prefer to emit the instance only when at least one filesystem of `fstype` `nfs`/`nfs4` is mounted.
notes: Despite the hint, the check does not read /proc/net/rpc/nfsd directly; it shells out to the `nfsiostat` Python script. Method `other` (no network probe). Linux-only in practice (the embedded binary is only meaningful there).
